subtopics.subtopic_description: Educational background or training
notes: Holds a Bachelor’s Degree from a regionally accredited university
Studied Arts Administration, with coursework in marketing and production management
Completed internships with local theaters during undergraduate studies
Attended several professional development workshops in event coordination and digital marketing
Participated in continuing education seminars on nonprofit arts management

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific job title and role description
notes: Job title: Marketing and Development Coordinator
Coordinates all aspects of event booking and production for a performing arts venue
Leads marketing initiatives, including design and promotion of events
Manages operational logistics and collaborates with production teams
Acts as primary liaison between artists, vendors, and internal departments

subtopics.subtopic_description: Current industry or sector (e.g., tech, finance, manufacturing)
notes: Works in the nonprofit performing arts sector
Industry focus is live performance and event management
Engaged in arts administration for a venue hosting concerts, theater, and community events

subtopics.subtopic_description: Company size and environment
notes: Performs duties at a midsize performing arts association with 20-40 employees
Organization has a collaborative, mission-driven culture
Office environment blends remote work with on-site event management
Frequent interaction with volunteers and part-time event staff

subtopics.subtopic_description: Type of business or market segment
notes: Nonprofit organization serving the local arts community
Market segment includes live entertainment, community engagement, and educational outreach
Revenue generated through ticket sales, grants, and sponsorships
Target audience ranges from general public to arts patrons and donors

subtopics.subtopic_description: Duration/years of experience in current role
notes: Has 3-5 years of experience as Marketing and Development Coordinator
Joined the association in early 2020
Previously held related roles in arts administration or event coordination

subtopics.subtopic_description: Professional seniority or career level
notes: Mid-level professional with organizational influence
Not part of executive leadership, but often leads cross-functional project teams
Has developed recognized expertise in event marketing and coordination

subtopics.subtopic_description: Primary job responsibilities and regular daily tasks
notes: Books and schedules performances, workshops, and rentals in a complex calendar system
Coordinates production needs: lighting, sound, staging, and artist requirements
Designs and distributes promotional materials, including social media content and videos
Handles operational logistics, such as contracts, vendor management, and facility setup
Monitors event budgets and collaborates with development staff on fundraising activities

subtopics.subtopic_description: Approximate proportion of time spent on core activities
notes: Spends approximately 40% of time managing event calendars and bookings
Allocates 30% to coordinating production and operations for upcoming events
Dedicates 25% to creating and executing marketing campaigns
Reserves about 5% for administrative duties and reporting

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of autonomy and scope of decision-making in the role
notes: Operates with significant autonomy in daily scheduling and marketing decisions
Makes independent choices on vendor selection, marketing channels, and minor budget allocations
Seeks executive approval for major expenditures and program changes
Acts as point of contact for resolving production or logistic issues during events

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that feel easiest or most natural to perform
notes: Quickly updates and manages event details in the calendar systems
Designs basic artwork and promotional graphics using provided templates
Communicates with artists and vendors to confirm logistics
Prepares standard email campaigns and social media posts

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks perceived as most challenging or complex
notes: Coordinating overlapping event schedules with limited resources
Managing last-minute production changes or technical issues
Developing innovative marketing strategies to boost underperforming events
Ensuring cross-team alignment under tight deadlines

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that are repetitive, data-heavy, or suitable for automation
notes: Entering event data into multiple calendar systems
Generating routine reports on ticket sales and attendance
Scheduling and posting recurring social media content
Updating website event listings with standardized information

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that are most enjoyable or engaging versus those that feel boring or tedious
notes: Enjoys brainstorming creative campaign ideas and designing visual assets
Feels engaged when collaborating on live event production
Finds routine calendar updates and data entry tedious
Enjoys interacting with artists and community partners

subtopics.subtopic_description: Common pain points or inefficiencies in completing tasks
notes: Experiences frustration with duplicative data entry across calendar systems
Encounters delays when event details change after marketing materials are finalized
Faces integration issues between marketing tools and the calendar platforms
Manual tracking of production requirements leads to occasional oversight

subtopics.subtopic_description: How enjoyment, skill level, and productivity relate to one another
notes: Productivity is highest when working on creative projects with clear outcomes
Skill level in digital marketing tools enhances efficiency and confidence
Less enjoyable, repetitive tasks tend to be completed slower and with less enthusiasm
Engagement and enjoyment increase when learning new design or video tools

subtopics.subtopic_description: Attitude towards learning new technologies and tools
notes: Generally open and curious about adopting new digital solutions
Views technology as a means to streamline workflows and enhance creativity
Prefers hands-on experimentation when learning software

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived adaptability to new software/methods
notes: Adapts quickly to updates in Microsoft 365 and Adobe tools
Comfortable exploring new calendar or marketing platforms
Occasionally needs additional time when tools have complex interfaces

subtopics.subtopic_description: Willingness to invest time in tech training
notes: Willing to dedicate a few hours per month to learn new tools
Attends webinars or online tutorials when relevant to current projects
Sometimes prioritizes immediate deadlines over in-depth tech training

subtopics.subtopic_description: Motivations or barriers to learning new tech (e.g., workload, relevance)
notes: Motivated to learn technology that directly saves time or improves marketing impact
High workloads can delay tech adoption or deeper exploration
Prefers technology with clear, demonstrable benefits for daily tasks
Occasional skepticism towards tools that seem overly complex for needs

subtopics.subtopic_description: Influence of peers or management on willingness to adopt new tools
notes: Peers often share useful tech tips, fostering a collaborative learning environment
Management encourages experimentation with new marketing and automation tools
Adopts tools more readily when given peer recommendations and use cases

topic: Primary Tools and Technologies Used in Work

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific software, platforms, or systems used daily
notes: Uses Microsoft 365 suite daily for email, document creation, and communication
Manages event logistics using four separate calendar systems
Relies on Adobe Acrobat for document editing and PDF management
Utilizes social media platforms (e.g., Facebook, Instagram) for event promotion

subtopics.subtopic_description: Essential non-AI tools for workflow
notes: Depends on shared drives and cloud storage for document collaboration
Uses group messaging apps (e.g., Microsoft Teams) for team coordination
Employs basic photo and video editing tools for content creation

subtopics.subtopic_description: Familiarity with industry-standard technologies
notes: Proficient in Microsoft 365 applications, including Outlook, Word, and Excel
Comfortable with Adobe Acrobat and basic design tools
Familiar with common event management and marketing platforms used in the arts sector

subtopics.subtopic_description: Interoperability or integration issues between tools
notes: Frequently encounters issues syncing events across multiple calendar platforms
Experiences workflow interruptions due to lack of integration between marketing and scheduling tools
Manual data transfer between systems sometimes leads to errors or duplication

subtopics.subtopic_description: Familiarity with fundamental AI/ML concepts and terminology
notes: Understands basic AI concepts such as natural language processing and generative models
Has moderate awareness of terms like 'large language models' and 'machine learning'
Knows how AI can automate content creation and data analysis in marketing

subtopics.subtopic_description: Names of specific AI software/platforms currently used in work
notes: Uses ChatGPT or similar LLMs to generate marketing copy and social media posts
Experiments with Canva's AI features for artwork and design suggestions
Has tested AI-powered video creation tools for event promos

subtopics.subtopic_description: Frequency and purpose of AI tool application (specific use cases)
notes: Applies AI tools on a weekly basis to create promotional content
Uses AI to draft emails and press releases for upcoming events
Leverages AI-generated imagery for social media campaigns
Employs AI to quickly brainstorm event themes and marketing taglines

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific examples of AI success and failure experiences (lessons learned)
notes: Achieved higher engagement rates with AI-generated social media captions
Noted occasional inaccuracies in AI-generated event descriptions, requiring manual edits
Successfully reduced turnaround time for video promos using AI-based editing tools
Learned to cross-check AI outputs for brand consistency and factual accuracy

subtopics.subtopic_description: Availability of organizational training or peer resources for AI use
notes: AI tool adoption is largely self-driven, with informal knowledge sharing among peers
No formal company training on AI yet, but staff are encouraged to experiment
Peers provide tips via chat and group meetings when new AI features are discovered

topic: AI Interaction Style and Workflow Change

subtopics.subtopic_description: Preferred mode of interaction: independent versus step-by-step collaboration
notes: Prefers step-by-step collaboration with AI tools for creative tasks
Often iterates on AI-generated drafts before finalizing content
Values the ability to prompt and guide AI outputs interactively

subtopics.subtopic_description: Style of human-AI teaming (e.g., advisor, assistant, co-worker)
notes: Uses AI primarily as an intelligent assistant for brainstorming and drafting
Treats AI as a creative partner in the initial stages of content development
Relies on human judgment for final approvals and edits

subtopics.subtopic_description: Willingness and openness to adopting new AI-driven workflows
notes: Open to integrating AI more deeply into routine marketing workflows
Actively seeks out new AI tools that could automate repetitive tasks
Maintains a cautious approach by testing AI outputs before full adoption

subtopics.subtopic_description: Preference for conversational vs. command-based interfaces (communication dynamics)
notes: Prefers conversational AI interfaces for brainstorming and content generation
Finds natural language prompts more intuitive than rigid command syntax
Occasionally uses command-based options for batch tasks or data processing

subtopics.subtopic_description: Extent to which tasks rely on specialized, tacit domain knowledge
notes: Many marketing tasks require nuanced understanding of the local arts audience
Event production planning depends on in-depth knowledge of venue capabilities
Certain content must reflect organizational tone and mission, which AI may not fully grasp

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of trust in AI outputs for work tasks and critical decisions
notes: Trusts AI-generated content for initial drafts, but always reviews before publishing
Limited trust in AI for complex scheduling or budget decisions
Relies on manual checks for any public-facing materials produced by AI

subtopics.subtopic_description: Ideal balance of human effort and AI automation for specific tasks
notes: Prefers AI automation for repetitive or routine content creation
Believes human oversight is essential for creative direction and final approvals
Sees potential for greater automation in data entry and report generation

subtopics.subtopic_description: Conditions under which high automation is acceptable or threatening
notes: Accepts high automation for standardized, low-risk tasks (e.g., scheduling reminders)
Finds automation less acceptable for tasks requiring brand sensitivity or crisis management
Concerned about loss of creative control if automation is applied too broadly

topic: AI Impact on Skills and Job Security

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived impact of AI on the importance of existing skills (enhanced vs. reduced)
notes: Sees AI as enhancing creative skills by freeing time for higher-level work
Believes strong communication and brand storytelling remain critical despite automation
Recognizes that technical skills in prompt engineering are becoming more important

subtopics.subtopic_description: Emerging skills or new areas of responsibility created by AI
notes: Developing proficiency in crafting effective AI prompts
Taking on responsibility for evaluating and editing AI-generated content
Exploring opportunities to integrate AI-driven analytics into marketing strategy

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of concern about AI replacing specific tasks or the overall role
notes: Mild concern about automation of repetitive marketing tasks
Does not believe core creative and coordination responsibilities are at risk
Views AI as a tool to support, rather than replace, the role

subtopics.subtopic_description: Availability of override mechanisms or manual checks for AI-driven processes
notes: Always reviews AI-generated materials before public release
Maintains manual control over scheduling and critical communications
Has protocols in place for human approval of any automated outputs

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived change in team or company policies regarding AI adoption
notes: Team is increasingly open to AI experimentation for marketing and content creation
No formal policy changes yet, but informal guidelines emphasize human oversight
Management has signaled support for responsible AI use in daily operations

subtopics.subtopic_description: General outlook on AI's broader societal and industry impact
notes: Believes AI will make arts marketing more efficient and accessible
Anticipates increased use of AI for audience engagement and data analysis
Recognizes potential for AI to democratize content creation, but warns of homogenization

subtopics.subtopic_description: Personal beliefs about the ethics and risks of AI in the workplace
notes: Supports transparency in AI-generated content and clear attribution
Is concerned about AI perpetuating biases in marketing or hiring materials
Advocates for responsible use of AI without replacing human jobs

subtopics.subtopic_description: Missing AI tools or features that would be most beneficial in the future
notes: Desires AI tools that seamlessly integrate all calendar and marketing platforms
Wishes for AI systems that can better capture organizational voice and style
Seeks automated analytics to provide actionable insights on audience engagement

subtopics.subtopic_description: Predicted evolution of their job in the next 5-10 years with AI integration
notes: Expects to spend less time on manual scheduling and more on strategic planning
Anticipates AI will handle most routine content creation and analytics
Believes creative direction and community relationship-building will remain human-led

subtopics.subtopic_description: Concrete steps they would want their organization to take regarding AI strategy
notes: Wants formal training sessions on emerging AI tools relevant to arts administration
Recommends piloting AI-integrated calendar and marketing solutions
Advocates for clear guidelines on ethical and responsible AI use
